learningObjectives.title: Understand Transportation Cockpit
learningObjectives.description: Explain the cockpit as central interface for planner-driven and optimizer-driven operations.

learningObjectives.title: Understand planning methods
learningObjectives.description: Differentiate manual, interactive, optimizer and background planning methods.

learningObjectives.title: Understand planning workflow
learningObjectives.description: Describe end-to-end cockpit flow from loading demand to saving freight plans.

sections.content: The Transportation Cockpit is the primary planning workspace in SAP Transportation Management. It provides transportation planners with a graphical interface to view Freight Units, Freight Orders, Resources and transportation demand, enabling manual, interactive and optimizer-based planning.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: The Transportation Cockpit is the central planning tool where transportation planners create, modify, optimize and monitor transportation plans.

sections.title: Planning Types
rows: Planning executed automatically in scheduled jobs

sections.content: A transportation planner receives 250 Freight Units for today's deliveries. Using the Transportation Cockpit, the planner reviews transportation demand, executes the optimizer, validates the proposed Freight Orders, assigns available trucks and releases shipments for execution.

sections.title: Best Practice
sections.content: Always review optimizer proposals before releasing Freight Orders. Business constraints or exceptional customer requirements may require manual adjustments.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: A logistics company uses the Transportation Cockpit to plan over 12,000 Freight Units daily. Optimizer-based planning reduced manual planning time by 65% while increasing truck utilization by 21%.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: Transportation Cockpit is one of the most frequently discussed SAP TM planning tools. Be prepared to explain its purpose, layout and planning methods.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: The Transportation Cockpit does not create transportation demand. Freight Units are created first, then loaded into the Cockpit for planning.

items: Transportation Cockpit is the planner's primary workspace.
It supports manual, interactive and optimizer-based planning.
Freight Units are loaded into the Cockpit for planning.
Freight Orders are created from planned Freight Units.
The Cockpit improves transportation planning efficiency and visibility.
